title: references/bootstrap.md — interactive 2-gate protocol + cold-start mining prompt
description:
FIRST: 'git merge main --no-edit'; verify kb bootstrap exists + references/{structure,extraction,claude-md-shim}.md.

Author skills/repo-wiki/references/bootstrap.md — the agent-facing interactive protocol for cold-starting a wiki in an existing repo. It is the heart of this epic. Sections:

1. GATE 1 — AGREE THE STRUCTURE (always MECE). Run 'kb bootstrap' for signals; propose the recommended MECE category set ADAPTED to this repo (e.g. add operations/ if service indicators; note an existing ADR dir → decisions/ will reference/synthesize, not duplicate). Present to the user (AskUserQuestion or conversation); let them edit/approve. Whatever is agreed MUST remain MECE (exactly-one-home) — if the user adds/renames, keep the resolver consistent. RECORD the agreed structure + any deviations in the root INDEX.md resolver. THEN scaffold via 'kb init'. Do not proceed to mining until structure is agreed.

2. GATE 2 — AGREE THE INGESTION SCOPE per source, using the COUNTS from 'kb bootstrap' so the user decides informed:
   - chats: all project history / last N days / none
   - commit messages: all / since <date|tag> / none
   - existing docs: which (README, docs/, ADR dir, CLAUDE.md)
   - codebase: which subsystems to map / cache
   Present the counts + options; capture the user's choices. Do not mine outside the agreed scope.

3. COLD-START MINING PROMPT (verbatim, applied after both gates): given the agreed sources + scope, produce a SMALL, STINGY starter set, propose-not-apply:
   - existing docs → migrate (reuse the CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md migration from claude-md-shim.md; generalize to README/docs/ADRs)
   - chats (scoped) → extraction.md Prompt 1 backfill over the in-scope sessions
   - commits (scoped) → mine decision-shaped commit messages into decisions/
   - code (scoped) → architecture orientation (map) + a few from-code caches for the named subsystems
   Route every item via the AGREED resolver; stamp covers + verified_against (current HEAD); be stingy (a few real pages, not exhaustive); MARK GAPS explicitly ('couldn't determine X — confirm with the team') rather than fabricate non-derivable truth.

4. REVIEW + COMMIT — present proposed pages; on approval, commit.

Cross-link from references map is in T3.

Test: bootstrap.md exists and documents the 2 gates (MECE structure agreement + recorded-in-resolver; scoped ingestion using kb bootstrap counts) and the mining prompt (all 4 sources, propose-not-apply, stingy, gap-marking, MECE routing) + review/commit.
notes: 2026-06-15 08:57 - [human] MINING FAN-OUT (add to the cold-start mining prompt, step 3): the agent must farm out maximal work to PARALLEL subagents so bootstrap stays fast + non-blocking. Dispatch one subagent per source (docs-migration / chat-backfill / commit-mining / code-orientation), and parallelize WITHIN a source where possible (one per code subsystem, one per chat-session-batch). Each subagent returns PROPOSED pages only (propose-not-apply) — the main agent collects + presents them for review. Keep the two GATES (structure, ingestion scope) in the interactive main thread with the user; only the mining fans out. Reference the 'heavy work off the main thread' guidance in references/activation.md + references/extraction.md (dispatch to subagents, don't mine inline).
acceptance_criteria: references/bootstrap.md documents GATE 1 (MECE structure agreement → resolver, then kb init), GATE 2 (per-source scope using kb bootstrap counts), the cold-start mining prompt (docs/chats/commits/code → proposed pages, propose-not-apply, stingy, gap-marked, MECE-routed), and review/commit
closed_reason: Completed: references/bootstrap.md (355 lines) — Step 0 kb bootstrap; GATE 1 MECE structure agreement→resolver→kb init; GATE 2 per-source scope using counts; Step 3 mining fanned out to parallel subagents (docs/chat-batches/commit/code-per-subsystem), propose-not-apply, stingy, gap-marked, MECE-routed; Step 4 review+commit; cross-refs.
